tickers.one_line_thesis: DataSection is betting the Neo Cloud franchise on hyperscaler-linked AI data centres in Japan (Inzai) and Australia, with a FY2026 revenue step-up to ¥33.6B still guided post-Apr-2026 delays; the May 2026 shift to NVIDIA B300 via Compal (~USD 325M) and a disclosed financing bridge improves execution visibility but raises contract-renegotiation, debt-timing, and Giga-order overhang risk.

Japan independent asset manager and ETF platform (Simplex Financial Holdings): high-margin fee croupier on domestic flows; at ¥464 and ~¥23/sh owner cash the seven-year expected return is 10.47% per year, below our hurdle, pending yuho ingest and live quote.

Japan's dominant exchange and clearing infrastructure — a Stahl croupier with ~58% operating margins and volume leverage — but earnings are cyclical with market turnover; do not treat FY2025 peak activity as a permanent baseline.
Altius Minerals Corporation — thesis pending Marvin deep dive.

Dominant e-commerce + AWS platform compounding at scale (FY2025 $717B sales, $78B NI) with AWS re-accelerating (+28% Q1-26) and high-margin ads/chips engines emerging — but AI capex has temporarily collapsed FCF, requiring disciplined valuation and capex ROI monitoring.

AI data centre developer with accelerating revenue ($126.6M FQ3, +139% YoY) and 600+ MW of signed leases (CoreWeave, hyperscaler). FQ3 GAAP net income $100.9M includes $59.7M discontinued-operation gain (Cloud held-for-sale); continuing ops still loss-making on adjusted basis. Customer concentration and layered debt/preferred stack require capital-structure diligence before conviction sizing.

Berkshire Hathaway Class B (BRK.B): fortress float and operating conglomerate at ~0.91x book, but ~6% Lawrence return on FY2025 operating earnings at $475 — watch until cheaper or faster capital deployment.

Global alternative-asset compounder earning durable fee streams on $614B fee-bearing capital with scaling insurance/wealth platform ($180B assets), active buybacks, and corporate simplification (BN+BNT merge) — DE-focused valuation, not GAAP NI.
Burford Capital Limited — thesis pending Marvin deep dive.
At ~$549, headline 3.07% per year (7yr blend) combines owner cash, scenarios, and NAV optionality; Lawrence cash alone is -1.4% while probability-weighted economic NAV is ~$1,480/sh.
Cboe Global Markets: Stahl croupier on options/equities/data; watch at ~$285 (~13.4% synthesis IRR); entry ~$250–255 for ~15% base.
CME Group is the dominant listed derivatives croupier; at $252.65 total synthesis is 12.01% per year on mid-cycle owner cash (watch; add band ~$210–220 illustrative).

CMSG is a cash-rich OTCQX bitcoin/scrypt miner where marked-to-market digital assets dominate GAAP earnings; the equity is largely a BTC-treasury and hash-share option under Horizon-affiliated governance — watch at 8.7% blended IRR (Marvin floor 7.7%); approved Substacks (LCI seigniorage) add partial treasury credit; fee redesign post-2026 and network-share trends.
Global salvage-vehicle auction toll booth de-rated to ~$33 — oligopoly economics intact; base IRR 9.2% (core override) while monitoring U.S. assignment/fee growth and Q3 FY2026 trends.
Wide-moat CRE platform de-rated to ~$33 (−54% YoY); Homes.com/Matterport distort GAAP — watch at 8.6% base IRR until normalized FCF proves out.
Decentralized VMS acquirer reinvesting FCFA2S at hurdle rates — base 16.0% IRR supports core stance; GAAP net income secondary to FCFA2S compounding.
DBS-driven life science/diagnostics platform — hold at 10.1% base IRR (quality override); cyclically tied to biopharma funding and China diagnostics recovery.
Passive oil and gas royalty MLP distributing ~100% of cash; 13.8% base IRR at $28/unit on FY2025 $2.78 distributions; watch below ~15% bar.
MAC-anchored ASX royalty at ~5% yield with Thacker Pass lithium option; 8.0% base five-year return at A$4.43; watch pending ASX doc pipeline and iron ore cycle.
Franco-Nevada: zero-debt gold royalty compounder at a quality premium; 4.5% base owner-cash return at $229 — watch for better entry or Cobre Panamá / commodity upside in overlay.

Horizon Kinetics–affiliated holding company with concentrated stakes in exchange infrastructure (MIH) and HKHC — a look-through bet on Murray Stahl's ecosystem, not an operating croupier; value = sum-of-parts vs fair-value accounting noise.

Galaxy Digital is an institutional crypto platform with growing fee lines and a Helios HPC option; at $28.48 the synthesis return is 7.65% per year (watch) until owner cash clears a 15% accumulate bar.

Search/YouTube ads cash engine plus Google Cloud AI infrastructure (+63% Q1 2026) — wide-moat compounder betting massive capex ($180–190B 2026) on Cloud ROI while navigating AI disruption to Search monetization.

Hawaiian Electric is a regulated Hawaii utility recovering from the 2023 Maui wildfire overhang; at ~$13 the stock offers a partial dhando recovery path but sub-15% base owner-cash return until dividend and normalized cash are visible.
Horizon Kinetics listed advisory parent: fee growth and book value, but spot price embeds a franchise premium; synthesis ~-4.81% at $27.50 — watch; size via FRMO look-through instead.

Global exchange and market-infrastructure croupier with 20 consecutive record revenue years and ~80% exchange margins — quality toll booth, but Lawrence base IRR ~11% at $156 sits below the 15% hold band on mid-cycle FCF; watch for pullback or bull-case volume.

Indiana Resources trades far below April 2025 cash per share; base case is a dated payoff (proposed A$0.05 return plus residual stub) implying ~42.4% per year at A$0.027, with Minos gold and legal paths as bull options.

India's regulated power-market croupier: high-quality toll on electricity, REC, and gas volume, but at ₹124.07 the 7-year synthesis return (9.74%) is below a core-add hurdle; watch until price or FY2025 earnings refresh improves the math.

Michigan mineral royalty optionality (>1M acres, Copperwood/Highland Copper leases) with patient balance sheet — asymmetric upside if mining develops, steady burn and single-tenant concentration until royalties activate.
Delaware Basin land toll with strong FY2025 cash proof, but Class A-attributable owner cash at ~$73 yields ~1.8% Lawrence base — watch for price or governance/cash-attribution improvement.
Global data/index/clearing croupier with 50%+ margins and strong cash conversion; quality franchise at ~£91 implies 12.6% base return — watch below 15% bar.
At ~$633, Meta's Family of Apps funds AI capex and Reality Labs; Lawrence base annual return 5.9% (watch stance) pending proof that $125–145B 2026 capex earns owner cash.
MIAX is a fast-growing U.S./Bermuda exchange croupier; at ~$40 normalized owner cash implies ~11% Lawrence IRR (13% synthesis) — watch until ~15% entry or futures share proof in filings.
Global insurance broker and consulting compounder: FY2025 revenue $27B, ~23% operating margin; at ~$157 Lawrence base ~17% annual return supports hold; dhando partial on leverage and cycle.

Mesabi Trust is a contractual iron ore pellet royalty on Cleveland-Cliffs' Northshore (Silver Bay, MN): cash is cyclical commodity + bonus-tier optionality, with ongoing AAA arbitration (commenced Sep 2025) layered on statutory trust simplicity—hold at 8.4% normalized yield for the incumbent royalty sleeve; do not downgrade on depressed Q1 bonus alone.
Strategy Inc. (MSTR): levered bitcoin treasury plus ~$463M analytics software; 762k BTC at Q1 2026; base 4.2%/yr look-through at ~$390; watch (software cash cannot justify price alone).
High-margin precious metals royalty company (OR Royalties) with ~81k GEOs today, filing-guided path to 120–135k GEOs by 2030, ~6% dividend yield, and watch stance at 5.98% synthesis return (~$35.31).

U.S. OTC market infrastructure and ATS operator — a Stahl croupier with subscription-heavy fees and expanding disclosure tiers (OTCID) and extended-hours trading — earnings scale with issuer count and activity, not balance-sheet risk.

Passive Permian royalty trust trading ~2.5× SEC reserve floor; Waddell Ranch in excess cost; base yield-curve path -15.4% over seven years — watch for cost recovery or conversion catalyst, not a yield buy at ~$29.
Pure Cycle: exclusive Denver-area water rights plus phased Sky Ranch lot economics; watch until Lawrence return clears 15% or overlay NAV is human-approved.
PrairieSky Royalty Ltd. — thesis pending Marvin deep dive.
Post-merger diagnostics platform (Quidel + Ortho) with improving adjusted margins but declining revenue — integration and non-respiratory growth must prove out before this is a compounder again.

Royal Gold is a high-margin precious-metals royalty franchise enlarged by the 2025 Sandstorm combination; blended 7-year return 8.06% at ~$217 (Lawrence cash path 6.7%) is below our accumulate bar — watch.
UK property portal monopoly at ~9% cash yield; base 23.3% annual return at £4.43 on FY2025 operating cash per share; accumulate with human review of FY2026 investment-year normalization.
Diversified pharma royalty aggregator; watch at ~$55 (~-0.9% Lawrence base, 7yr) until price or distributable cash improves.

Sabine Royalty Trust passes through monthly oil and gas royalties with minimal overhead; at ~$77 we see ~6% trailing yield and 7.3% base annual return on a five-year distribution curve, watch below the ~15% bar.

Passive San Juan Basin gas NPI royalty trust — equity yield curve / time arbitrage on temporary Hilcorp capex deficit (no distributions since May 2024); re-rating potential when distributions resume if unit price discounts recoverable NPI rather than terminal wind-down.
AI Data Cloud platform on consumption pricing: strong product growth and positive FCF, but watch at ~$240 (~6.7% base IRR) until owner cash per share proves at post-earnings run rate.

Essential financial intelligence — ratings, indices, and data subscriptions — a Stahl croupier / index infrastructure compounder with extraordinary margins; earnings leverage to issuance and AUM with cyclicality at the margin.

Swedish industrial serial acquirer of niche subsidiaries — decentralized Pabrai-style model with 2025–2026 margin/FCF turnaround underway; organic sales still negative while unprofitable units are pruned.
Permian land, royalty, and water toll at ~$393: durable moat, falsifier-adjusted base -1.0%, NAV overlay ~$211/sh vs price — watch for better entry.
~270,000 California acres and entitlements (Centennial, Grapevine, TRCC) at ~$18.84 near GAAP book; base 5.8% per year on 7-year dated NAV payoff — watch until IRR or catalyst tightens.

Post-EWSR generic pharma; 13.61% synthesis IRR ($1.50/sh 50/50 Marvin/Rosen); floor 9.9% on $1.45. Rosen Sohn: Idorsia Phase III + cash-yield re-rating (catalyst capped, not 12mo double in base). ~$14B net debt; watch.

Delaware Basin produced-water infrastructure with strong Adj. EBITDA growth ($254M FY2025) and ~2,500 miles of pipe — asset-heavy midstream toll model, not Stahl croupier; thesis blocked by IPO/combination complexity and Five Point sponsor control.
Precious-metals streamer with record FY2025 cash generation; at ~$125 the base return is below our accumulate bar — watch until mid-cycle owner cash or price offers ~15% annual return.
